problem_statement: Consider a circle Γ with center O and a line l not intersecting Γ. Prove there is a unique point Q on the perpendicular from O to l such that for any point P on l, PQ equals the length of the tangent from P to Γ.

Let ABCD be a quadrilateral. X, Y are the midpoints of AC and BD; the lines through X parallel to BD and through Y parallel to AC meet at O. P, Q, R, S are the midpoints of AB, BC, CD, DA. Prove that APOS and APXS have equal area, and that APOS, BQOP, CROQ, DSOR all have equal area.
In triangle ABC, D is the midpoint of BC. If angle ADB = 45° and angle ACD = 30°, determine angle BAD.

Let ABC be an acute triangle and D, E, F the feet of the perpendiculars from A, B, C to BC, CA, AB. The perpendiculars from F to CB, CA, AD, BE meet them at P, Q, M, N. Prove that P, Q, M, N are collinear.
Let ABC be an acute triangle; D, F the midpoints of BC, AB. The perpendicular from F to AC and the perpendicular to BC at B meet at N. Prove that ND equals the circumradius of ABC.
Let ABC be a triangle with angle A = 60°. BE and CF are the bisectors of angles B and C, with E on AC and F on AB. Let M be the reflection of A in line EF. Prove that M lies on BC.
In an acute triangle ABC with angle A = 30°, H is the orthocentre and M the midpoint of BC. On line HM take T with HM = MT. Show that AT = 2 BC.
The incircle of triangle ABC touches BC, CA, AB at K, L, M. The line through A parallel to LK meets MK at P; the line through A parallel to MK meets LK at Q. Show that PQ bisects AB and AC.
In acute triangle ABC let P be an interior point. Lines BP and CP meet AC and AB at E and F. Let D = AP ∩ EF and K the foot of the perpendicular from D to BC. Show that DK bisects angle EKF.

Let ABC be an acute triangle with altitude AK, orthocentre H, circumcentre O. Suppose KOH is acute with circumcentre P. Let Q be the reflection of P in line HO. Show that Q lies on the line joining the midpoints of AB and AC.

Let ABC be a triangle with circumcircle Γ. Let M be an interior point on the bisector of angle A. AM, BM, CM meet Γ again at A1, B1, C1. Let P = A1C1 ∩ AB and Q = A1B1 ∩ AC. Prove that PQ is parallel to BC.
A segment AB has a point M on it. On the same side, squares AMCD and BMFE are built. The circumcircles of the two squares, with centres P and Q, meet at M and N. Prove that lines FA and BC meet at N.

The incircle is inscribed in triangle ABC. Three tangents to the incircle are drawn, each parallel to one side, forming three smaller triangles, each with its own incircle. Determine the sum of areas of all four incircles.

Given triangle OAB with angle AOB = α < 90°, let M be a point on side AB. P and Q are the feet of the perpendiculars from M to OA and OB. Let H be the orthocentre of triangle OPQ. Find the locus of H.
Let M, K, L be points on AB, BC, CA of triangle ABC. Prove that at least one of the triangles MAL, KBM, LCK has area at most one quarter of the area of ABC.
Points A, B, C, D lie on a circle with AB a diameter and CD not. The tangents at C and D meet at P, and AC and BD meet at Q. Show that PQ is perpendicular to AB.

Two circles touch internally; an equilateral triangle is inscribed in the larger circle. From each vertex a segment tangent to the smaller circle is drawn. Prove that one tangent length equals the sum of the other two.
Let ABCD be a cyclic quadrilateral. Show that the centroids of triangles ABC, CDA, BCD, DAB lie on a circle.
Prove that the perpendiculars from the midpoints of the sides of a cyclic quadrilateral to the opposite sides are concurrent.
A line l passes through the orthocentre H of an acute triangle ABC. Prove that the reflections of l in the sides BC, CA, AB have a common point lying on the circumcircle of ABC.
A line meets sides AB and AC of triangle ABC at X and Y with BX = CY. Find the locus of the circumcentre of triangle XAY.

A right-angled triangle OAB has its right angle at the point B. An arbitrary circle with center on the line OB is tangent to the line OA. Let AT be the tangent to the circle different from OA (T is the point of tangency). Prove that the median from B of the triangle OAB intersects AT at a point M such that MB = MT.

In a right triangle ABC we have angle A = 90 deg, angle C = 30 deg. Denote by the circle passing through A which is tangent to BC at the midpoint. Assume that it intersects AC and the circumcircle of ABC at N and M respectively. Prove that MN is perpendicular to BC.

In an acute triangle ABC, the circle with diameter BC meets AB, AC at E, F. M is the midpoint of BC and P = AM ∩ EF. X is a point on arc EF and Y the second intersection of XP with the circle. Show that angle XAY = angle XYM.

The tangent line to the circumcircle of acute-angled triangle ABC (AC > AB) at A intersects the continuation of BC at P. O is the circumcenter of ABC. X is a point on OP such that angle AXP = 90 deg. Points E, F on AB, AC are chosen so that angle EXP = angle ACX and angle FXO = angle ABX. If K, L are the intersections of EF with the circumcircle of ABC, show that OP is tangent to the circumcircle of triangle KLX.

Points P, Q lie on side BC of triangle ABC, equidistant from its midpoint. The perpendiculars to BC at P, Q meet AC, AB at E, F. Let M = PF ∩ EQ. If H1, H2 are the orthocentres of triangles BFP and CEQ, show that AM is perpendicular to H1H2.

Suppose that I is the incenter of triangle ABC and CI intersects AB at D. In the circumcircle of ABC, T is the midpoint of arc BAC and BI intersects this circle at M. If MD intersects AT at N, prove that BM is parallel to CN.
In acute triangle ABC, BH is the altitude from B. D, E are the midpoints of AB, CA. Let F be the reflection of H across line ED. Prove that line BF passes through the circumcentre of ABC.

In triangle ABC, M, N, K are the midpoints of BC, CA, AB. omegaB and omegaC are the semicircles with diameters CA and AB, outside the triangle. MK meets omegaC at X, MN meets omegaB at Y. The tangents at X and Y meet at Z. Prove that AZ is perpendicular to BC.

In acute triangle ABC, angle A = 45 deg. Points O, H are the circumcenter and the orthocenter of ABC. D is the foot of the altitude from B. Point X is the midpoint of arc AH of the circumcircle of triangle ADH that doesn't contain D. Prove that DX = DO.

Two circles ω1, ω2 (centres O1, O2) intersect at A and B. X lies on ω2. Y is on ω1 with angle XBY = 90°. Let X' be the second intersection of line O1X with ω2 and K the second intersection of X'Y with ω2. Prove that X is the midpoint of arc AK.

Let H be the orthocentre of triangle ABC. Two perpendicular lines l1, l2 pass through H. l1 meets BC and line AB at D and Z; l2 meets BC and line AC at E and X. Let Y satisfy YD parallel to AC and YE parallel to AB. Prove X, Y, Z are collinear.

Rectangles ABA1B2, BCB1C2, CAC1A2 lie outside triangle ABC. C' satisfies C'A1 perpendicular to A1C2 and C'B2 perpendicular to B2C1; A' and B' are defined analogously. Prove that lines AA', BB', CC' are concurrent.
In trapezoid ABCD with AB parallel to CD, ω1 and ω2 are the circles with diameters AD and BC. For arbitrary points X on ω1 and Y on ω2, show that XY is at most half the perimeter of ABCD.

Let ω be the circumcircle of right triangle ABC (angle A = 90°). The tangent to ω at A meets line BC at P. M is the midpoint of the smaller arc AB, and PM meets ω again at Q. The tangent to ω at Q meets AC at K. Prove that angle PKC = 90°.

Let the circles omega and omega' intersect at A and B. The tangent to omega at A intersects omega' at C and the tangent to omega' at A intersects omega at D. The internal bisector of angle CAD intersects omega and omega' at E and F, and the external bisector intersects omega and omega' at X and Y. Prove that the perpendicular bisector of XY is tangent to the circumcircle of triangle BEF.
Two circles ω1, ω2 intersect at A, B. A line through B meets ω1, ω2 at C, D. Points E on ω1, F on ω2 satisfy CE = CB and BD = DF. BF meets ω1 at P, BE meets ω2 at Q. Prove that A, P, Q are collinear.

In acute triangle ABC, the altitude from A meets BC at D, and M is the midpoint of AC. X is a point with angle AXB = angle DXM = 90° (X and C on opposite sides of line BM). Show that angle XMB = 2 angle MBC.
Let ABCD be a square and M, N points on sides AB, BC with angle MDN = 45°. If R is the midpoint of MN, show that RP = RQ, where P, Q are the intersections of AC with lines MD and ND.
ABCD is a quadrilateral; P, Q are the midpoints of CD, AB. AP and DQ meet at X; BP and CQ meet at Y. Prove that area(ADX) + area(BCY) = area(PXQY).
ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral with AC perpendicular to BD, meeting at E. Prove that EA² + EB² + EC² + ED² = 4R², where R is the circumradius.

Let B be a point between A and C on segment AC. On one side, isosceles triangles PAB and QBC are built with angle APB = angle BQC = 120°; on the other side, isosceles triangle RAC with angle ARC = 120°. Show that PQR is equilateral.

BE and CF are the altitudes of acute triangle ABC (E on AC, F on AB), meeting at O. A line KL through O has K on AB and L on AC. M on BE and N on CF satisfy KM perpendicular to BE and LN perpendicular to CF. Prove that FM is parallel to EN.

In the isosceles triangle ABC (AB = AC), let l be a line parallel to BC through A. Let D be an arbitrary point on l. Let E, F be the feet of perpendiculars from A to BD, CD respectively. Suppose P, Q are the reflections of E, F across l. Prove that AP + AQ <= AB.

construction_steps.name: l_seg
construction_steps.command: Segment(A - 6 UnitVector(Vector(B, C)), A + 10 UnitVector(Vector(B, C)))

problem_statement: In a convex quadrilateral ABCD, the lines AB and CD meet at E and the lines AD and BC meet at F. Let P be the intersection of diagonals AC and BD. omega1 is a circle through D tangent to AC at P; omega2 is a circle through C tangent to BD at P. X is the intersection of omega1 and AD, Y is the intersection of omega2 and BC. The circles omega1 and omega2 intersect again at Q. Prove that the perpendicular from P to EF passes through the circumcenter of triangle XQY.

Let X, Y be points on side BC of triangle ABC such that 2 XY = BC (X between B, Y). Let AA' be the diameter of the circumcircle of triangle AXY. Let P be the intersection of AX with the perpendicular from B to BC, and Q the intersection of AY with the perpendicular from C to BC. Prove that the tangent to the circumcircle of AXY at A' passes through the circumcenter of triangle APQ.

In triangle ABC, the incircle with center I touches BC at D. Line DI meets AC at X. The tangent from X to the incircle (different from AC) meets AB at Y. If YI and BC intersect at Z, prove that AB = BZ.

construction_steps.name: I
construction_steps.label: I
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(B, A, C), AngleBisector(A, B, C))

problem_statement: Let O be the circumcentre of triangle ABC. Line CO meets the altitude from A at K. P, M are the midpoints of AK, AC. If PO meets BC at Y, and the circumcircle of triangle BCM meets AB at X, prove that B, X, O, Y are concyclic.

Circles ω1, ω2 with centres O1, O2 intersect at A and B. Line O1B meets ω2 again at C; line O2A meets ω1 again at D. X is the second intersection of AC with ω1; Y the second intersection of BD with ω2. Prove that CX = DY.

Suppose ABCD is a parallelogram with angle DAC = 90 deg. Let H be the foot of the perpendicular from A to DC. Let P be a point on line AC such that PD is tangent to the circumcircle of triangle ABD. Prove that angle PBA = angle DBH.
Let ABC be a triangle and P, Q, R points on BC, CA, AB. Prove that the circumcircles of triangles AQR, BRP, CPQ have a common point.

On the sides of triangle ABC, triangles ABR, BCQ, CAP are built externally with angle CBQ = angle CAP = 45°, angle BCQ = angle ACP = 30°, angle ABR = angle BAR = 15°. Prove that angle QRP = 90° and QR = RP.

Let A be one of the two intersection points of two unequal circles C1, C2 with centers O1, O2. One common tangent touches C1 at P1 and C2 at P2; the other touches C1 at Q1 and C2 at Q2. Let M1, M2 be the midpoints of P1Q1 and P2Q2. Prove that angle O1AO2 = angle M1AM2.
Let ABCD be a convex quadrilateral with CD tangent to the circle on AB as diameter. Prove that AB is tangent to the circle on CD as diameter if and only if BC and AD are parallel.

A circle with centre O passes through vertices A and C of triangle ABC and meets segments AB, BC again at K, N. The circumcircles of triangles ABC and KBN meet again at M (besides B). Prove that angle OMB is a right angle.
Let A, B, C be three points. The perpendicular bisector of AB meets line BC at D, that of BC meets AC at E, and that of AC meets AB at F.

In acute triangle ABC the interior bisector of angle A meets BC at L and the circumcircle at N. From L, perpendiculars to AB and AC have feet K and M. Prove that the area of quadrilateral AKNM equals the area of triangle ABC.

ABC is a triangle right-angled at A, and D is the foot of the altitude from A. The line joining the incenters of triangles ABD and ACD meets AB, AC at K, L. S and T are the areas of triangles ABC and AKL. Show that S >= 2 T.

construction_steps.name: I1
construction_steps.label: I₁
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(B, A, D), AngleBisector(A, B, D))

construction_steps.name: I2
construction_steps.label: I₂
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(C, A, D), AngleBisector(A, C, D))

problem_statement: In acute triangle ABC, the internal bisector of angle A meets the circumcircle at A1 (B1, C1 similar). A0 is the intersection of line AA1 with the external bisectors of B and C (B0, C0 similar). Prove that area(A0B0C0) is twice area(AC1BA1CB1), and perimeter(A0B0C0) is at least twice the hexagon's perimeter.

construction_steps.name: poly_hexagon
construction_steps.command: Polygon(A, C1, B, A1, C, B1)

problem_statement: Let ABC be a triangle and P an interior point. Show that at least one of the angles PAB, PBC, PCA is at most 30°.

Let ABC be isosceles with AB = AC. M is the midpoint of BC and O is the point on line AM with OB perpendicular to AB. Q is a point on segment BC. E on line AB and F on line AC are collinear with Q. Prove that OQ is perpendicular to EF if and only if QE = QF.
Let P be a point inside triangle ABC such that angle APB - angle ACB = angle APC - angle ABC. Let D, E be the incenters of triangles APB, APC respectively. Show that AP, BD, CE meet at a point.

construction_steps.name: D
construction_steps.label: D
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(P, A, B), AngleBisector(A, P, B))

construction_steps.name: E
construction_steps.label: E
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(P, A, C), AngleBisector(A, P, C))

problem_statement: Angle A is the smallest in triangle ABC. U is an interior point of the arc BC not containing A. The perpendicular bisectors of AB and AC meet line AU at V and W. Lines BV and CW meet at T. Show that AU = TB + TC.
Let I be the incenter of triangle ABC. The incircle touches BC, CA, AB at K, L, M. The line through B parallel to MK meets lines LM and LK at R and S. Prove that angle RIS is acute.

Let ABC be a triangle. The circle through C tangent to AB at A meets BC at D; the circle through B tangent to AC at A meets BC at E. Line AD meets the second circle again at F, line AE meets the first again at G.

Let A, B, C, D be four points on a line in that order. The circles with diameters AC and BD meet at X and Y. XY meets BC at Z. P is a point on XY other than Z. Line CP meets the circle on AC again at M, and line BP meets the circle on BD again at N. Prove that AM, DN, XY are concurrent.

Let AH1, BH2, CH3 be the altitudes of an acute triangle ABC. The incircle touches BC, CA, AB at T1, T2, T3. Let L1, L2, L3 be the reflections of lines H2H3, H3H1, H1H2 in lines T2T3, T3T1, T1T2. Prove that L1, L2, L3 form a triangle whose vertices lie on the incircle of ABC.

Let ABC be a triangle with centroid G. Let A', B', C' be points on BC, CA, AB such that GA' is perpendicular to BC, GB' is perpendicular to CA, GC' is perpendicular to AB. Prove that A'B'C' is the image of ABC under a homothety centered at G.

A line l does not meet a circle omega with center O. E is the foot of the perpendicular from O to l. M is any point on l other than E. The tangents from M to omega touch it at A and B. C is the foot of the perpendicular from E to MA, and D the foot from E to MB. Line CD meets OE at F. Prove that F is independent of M.

Let ABC be a triangle with angle B > 90°, and H a point on AC with AH = BH and BH perpendicular to BC. D, E are the midpoints of AB, BC. The parallel to AB through E meets BH at F. Prove that angle BFD = angle ACB.
Let S be a point on the circumcircle of triangle ABC. Let SA, SB, SC be the reflections of S in the sides BC, CA, AB. Show that SA, SB, SC are collinear (the Steiner line).

Let ABC be an acute triangle with AB != AC. The circle with diameter BC meets AB, AC at M, N. O is the midpoint of BC. The bisectors of angle BAC and angle MON meet at R. Prove that the circumcircles of triangles BMR and CNR have a common point on BC.

Let ABCD be a convex quadrilateral with BC = AD and BC not parallel to AD. E, F are interior points of BC, AD with BE = DF. AC and BD meet at P; BD and EF meet at Q; EF and AC meet at R. Show that the circumcircles of triangles PQR, as E, F vary, share a common point other than P.
Let ABC be a triangle with incenter I. A point P satisfies angle PBA + angle PCA = angle PBC + angle PCB. Show that AP >= AI, with equality iff P = I. (P lies on the circle through B, I, C.)

construction_steps.name: I
construction_steps.label: I
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(B, A, C), AngleBisector(A, B, C))

problem_statement: Let I be the incenter of triangle ABC and Gamma its circumcircle. Line AI meets Gamma again at D. Let E be a point on Gamma and F a point on BC such that angle BAF = angle CAE. Let G be the midpoint of IF. Prove that lines DG and EI intersect on Gamma.

construction_steps.name: I
construction_steps.label: I
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(B, A, C), AngleBisector(A, B, C))

problem_statement: Given triangle ABC, J is the centre of the excircle opposite A. It touches BC at M and lines AB, AC at K, L. Lines LM and BJ meet at F; lines KM and CJ meet at G. Let S = AF ∩ BC and T = AG ∩ BC. Prove that M is the midpoint of ST.

Let ABC be an acute triangle with orthocentre H, and W a point on side BC. M, N are the feet of the altitudes from B and C. w1 is the circumcircle of BWN, w2 of CWM. X is the antipode of W on w1, Y the antipode of W on w2. Prove that X, Y, H are collinear.

Let P, Q be on side BC of acute triangle ABC with angle PAB = angle BCA and angle CAQ = angle ABC. M, N are on rays AP, AQ with P the midpoint of AM and Q the midpoint of AN. Prove that BM and CN meet on the circumcircle of ABC.

Let ABC be an acute triangle with AB > AC, circumcircle Gamma, orthocenter H, and F the foot of the altitude from A. Let M be the midpoint of BC. Q is the point on Gamma with angle HQA = 90 deg, and K is the point on Gamma with angle HKQ = 90 deg. Prove that the circumcircles of triangles KQH and FKM are tangent.

construction_steps.name: H
construction_steps.label: H
construction_steps.command: Intersect(PerpendicularLine(A, Line(B, C)), PerpendicularLine(B, Line(C, A)))

problem_statement: Let Gamma be the circumcircle of acute triangle ABC. D, E lie on segments AB, AC with AD = AE. The perpendicular bisectors of BD and CE meet the minor arcs AB and AC of Gamma at F and G. Prove that DE and FG are parallel (or coincide).

Let I be the incenter of acute triangle ABC with AB != AC. The incircle omega touches BC, CA, AB at D, E, F. The line through D perpendicular to EF meets omega at R. Line AR meets omega again at P. The circumcircles of triangles PCE and PBF meet again at Q. Prove that lines DI and PQ are parallel.

Let D be an interior point of acute triangle ABC with AB > AC, and I the incenter. Line AD meets BC at E. The line through E perpendicular to AI meets line DI at F. Prove that the reflection of F across AI lies on BC.

construction_steps.name: I
construction_steps.label: I
construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(B, A, C), AngleBisector(A, B, C))
